Examples

  • Gallery

rect

The rect mark emits one <rect> per row. It accepts either an offset form (x, y place the top-left corner, w/h are pixel sizes) or an interval form (x/x2 and y/y2 give two edges) — extentOf resolves which upstream. It is container-eligible: a rect's box can host a nested plot, which is the one mechanism behind faceting, small multiples, and nesting. Paired with the bandX + barY layouts it draws bar charts.

const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.rect("penguins", {
  x: linear("bill_length_mm"),
  y: linear("body_mass_g"),
  w: 10, h: 10,
  fill: ordinal("species"),
});
return v;

A "squared scatter" — same encoding as a dot plot, but each point is a 10×10 pixel box coloured by species.

Options

Supply positions as offset (x,y,w,h) or interval (x,x2,y,y2); mixing is resolved by extentOf. Bare strings are literals — use field(...) or a scale to bind a column.

channel kind default meaning
x positional-x — left edge (offset) or first x edge (interval)
y positional-y — top edge (offset) or first y edge (interval)
x2 positional-x — second x edge (interval form)
y2 positional-y — second y edge (interval form)
w pixel — width in pixels (offset form)
h pixel — height in pixels (offset form)
fill color black interior paint
stroke color none outline paint
strokeWidth pixel — outline width
strokeDasharray text — SVG dash pattern
opacity number 1 whole-mark opacity
fillOpacity number 1 interior opacity
strokeOpacity number 1 outline opacity
title text — per-mark hover tooltip; also a via join carrier

w, h, and strokeWidth are pixel channels (w→x axis, h→y axis).

Examples

Bar chart — bandX × barY. A bar needs four positional values from two multi-output layouts: bandX("species") gives {x, w} (band position + width), barY("mass") gives {y, h} (bar top + height to the baseline). The aggregation is modelled inline.

const bySpecies = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species"], { mass: avg("body_mass_g") });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.rect(bySpecies, {
  ...bandX("species", { paddingInner: 0.2 }).pick("x", "w"),  // gap between bars
  ...barY("mass").pick("y", "h"),
  fill: ordinal("species"),
});
return v;

Stacked bars. bandX places the bars; stackY partitions each bar's total and cumulates the segments (a windowed running sum) up the height. group = the bar, order = the stacking sequence.

const stacks = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species", "island"], { n: count() });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.rect(stacks, {
  ...bandX("species", { paddingInner: 0.2 }).pick("x", "w"),
  ...stackY({ weight: "n", group: "species", order: "island" }).pick("y", "h"),
  fill: ordinal("island"),
});
return v;

Interval form — a heat cell / range box. Give both edges instead of a size; here a manual histogram bar spans [x, x2] on x and [y, y2] on y.

const bins = db.derive("bins", `
  SELECT floor(bill_length_mm) AS x, floor(bill_length_mm) + 1 AS x2,
         0 AS y, count(*) AS y2
  FROM penguins GROUP BY 1, 2`);
const v = canvas(640, 460);
const x = linear();
const y = linear();
v.rect(bins, { x: x("x"), x2: x("x2"), y: y("y"), y2: y("y2"), fill: "#4e79a7" });
return v;

Container — nesting a keyed child. The rect is an encoded plot: each species box is coloured by a scale and hosts a nested dot plot keyed on species. nest() opens the template; add as many keyed layers as you like.

const species = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species"], {});  // one row per species
const v = canvas(760, 260);
const cell = v.rect(species,
    { ...eqX("species").pick("x", "w"), y: 12, h: 236, fill: ordinal("species") })
  .nest("penguins", { on: eq("species", "species") });
cell.dot({ x: linear("bill_length_mm"), y: linear("body_mass_g"), fill: "#ffffff" });
return v;

Small multiples (faceting). The .dot(...) chained on a container rect is shorthand for a single keyed nested layer. Shared scales make every panel's range identical.

const species = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species"], {});
const v = canvas(760, 260);
v.rect(species, { ...eqX("species").pick("x", "w"), y: 12, h: 236, fill: "#f5f6fa" })
 .dot("penguins",
   { x: linear("bill_length_mm"), y: linear("body_mass_g"), fill: "#4e79a7" },
   { on: eq("species", "species") });
return v;

Free (per-panel) scales. shared: false trains a domain per panel, so each panel's axis spans its own species' range and the points fill every panel.

const species = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species"], {});
const v = canvas(760, 260);
v.rect(species, { ...eqX("species").pick("x", "w"), y: 12, h: 236, fill: "#f5f6fa" })
 .dot("penguins",
   { x: linear("bill_length_mm", { shared: false }),
     y: linear("body_mass_g",    { shared: false }), fill: "#59a14f" },
   { on: eq("species", "species") });
return v;

Treemap. The treemap layout solves x,y,w,h per row from a measure; the rect draws each tile.

const revenue = db.table("orders").rollup(
  db.table("products"), { left: "product_id", right: "id" },
  "category", { amount: sum({ sql: "qty * price" }) });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.rect(revenue, {
  ...treemap("amount").pick("x", "y", "w", "h"),
  fill: ordinal("category"),
  stroke: "#fff",
});
return v;

See also

  • bar — an alias of rect with the same channels; use for column charts
  • cell — a positioned box with no explicit size (heatmap tile)
  • bandX — category → {x, w}
  • barY — value → {y, h} (bar top + height to baseline)
  • stackY — cumulative segments within a band
  • treemap — squarified rectangles sized by a measure
